current_revision.content:
The operator encodes Mode × Domain. The Object class appears as a coordinate badge: - (Condition) · + (Entity) · * (Pattern). Each position is fully specified by either OPERATOR(Object) or its symbol, and carries a canonical coordinate address (Mode, Domain, Object).

Axis coordinates:

  • Mode: 0 (Differentiating) · 1 (Relating) · 2 (Generating)

  • Domain: −1 (Existence) · +1 (Structure) · √2 (Significance)

  • Object: 2 (Condition) · √2 (Entity) · 2^√2 (Pattern)

Complete Sequence

#

Address (Mode, Domain, Object)

(0, −1, 2)

(1, −1, 2)

(2, −1, 2)

(0, +1, 2)

(2, √2, 2^√2)

What Each Position Means

NUL — marking absence

  • ∅- (0, −1, 2) — a field has cleared; a background state is gone

  • ∅+ (0, −1, √2) — a specific thing is not present; classical negation

  • ∅* (0, −1, 2^√2) — a regularity has dissolved; a structural order has ceased

SEN — marking presence

  • ⊡- (1, −1, 2) — a background state has become detectable; a field is active

  • ⊡+ (1, −1, √2) — a specific thing is registered as present; recognition

  • ⊡* (1, −1, 2^√2) — a regularity is in effect; a structure is operative

INS — instantiating

  • △- (2, −1, 2) — a background is established; a field is opened

  • △+ (2, −1, √2) — a specific thing is brought into being; creation

  • △* (2, −1, 2^√2) — a regularity is established; a structure is made to recur

SEG — segmenting

  • ǀ- (0, +1, 2) — an ambient field is partitioned into regions

  • ǀ+ (0, +1, √2) — a bounded thing is divided into distinguishable parts

  • ǀ* (0, +1, 2^√2) — sub-patterns are distinguished within a larger regularity

CON — connecting

  • ⋈- (1, +1, 2) — ambient fields are linked; background states placed in relation

  • ⋈+ (1, +1, √2) — specific things are related to each other

  • ⋈* (1, +1, 2^√2) — regularities are related; structural correspondences established

SYN — synthesizing

  • ∨- (2, +1, 2) — connected conditions produce a new emergent background state

  • ∨+ (2, +1, √2) — connected entities produce a new composite whole

  • ∨* (2, +1, 2^√2) — connected patterns produce a higher-order regularity

EVA — alternating

  • ∿- (0, √2, 2) — this background state is marked as distinct from that one

  • ∿+ (0, √2, √2) — this entity carries a different meaning from that one

  • ∿* (0, √2, 2^√2) — this paradigm is marked as distinct from that one

DEF — superposing

  • ∥- (1, √2, 2) — multiple background states are simultaneously operative

  • ∥+ (1, √2, √2) — an entity holds multiple simultaneous interpretations

  • ∥* (1, √2, 2^√2) — multiple paradigms are held as simultaneously valid

REC — recursing

  • ↬- (2, √2, 2) — a condition takes itself as its own ground; recursive ambient self-reference

  • ↬+ (2, √2, √2) — an entity models itself; reflexive self-awareness

  • ↬* (2, √2, 2^√2) — a pattern encompasses its own patterning; recursive systemic self-organization

Note on SEG Symbol

SEG's symbol | is the markdown table column delimiter and will render as a blank cell in table contexts. The substitute ǀ (U+01C0, latin letter dental click) is used throughout. In non-table prose, | is fine.
